title: Getting Started with sqlserverconnect
text: sqlserverconnect provides minimal wrapper around DBI, odbc, pool make connecting Microsoft SQL Server painless possible. package exports three functions: db_connect() – open DBI connection connection pool db_disconnect() – safely close either one db_connection_args() – build connection arguments list without actually connecting (useful debugging)

title: Getting Started with sqlserverconnect
text: common setup corporate environments. Windows passes credentials automatically, don’t need supply username password. Just set trusted = TRUE (default):
code: library(sqlserverconnect) library(DBI)  conn <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master" )  DBI::dbGetQuery(conn, "SELECT TOP (5) name, create_date FROM sys.databases")  db_disconnect(conn)

title: Getting Started with sqlserverconnect
text: SQL auth, set trusted = FALSE provide uid pwd. Avoid hardcoding passwords — use environment variables, keyring package, another secret manager. can set environment variables .Renviron file: restart R take effect.
code: conn <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   uid      = Sys.getenv("SQLSERVER_UID"),   pwd      = Sys.getenv("SQLSERVER_PWD"),   trusted  = FALSE )  DBI::dbGetQuery(conn, "SELECT TOP (5) name FROM sys.tables")  db_disconnect(conn) SQLSERVER_UID=my_username SQLSERVER_PWD=my_password

title: Getting Started with sqlserverconnect
text: want see exactly db_connect() pass DBI::dbConnect() without actually opening connection, use db_connection_args(): handy debugging driver issues verifying connection string.
code: args <- db_connection_args(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   trusted  = TRUE )  str(args)

title: Getting Started with sqlserverconnect
text: SQL Server defaults port 1433. instance uses different port, pass directly:
code: conn <- db_connect(   server   = "myserver",   database = "mydb",   port     = 1434 )

title: Getting Started with sqlserverconnect
text: named instances, include instance name server argument:
code: conn <- db_connect(   server   = "myserver\\SQLEXPRESS",   database = "mydb" )

what: Why use connection pools in Shiny?
title: Using sqlserverconnect with Shiny
text: plain DBI connection fine scripts, Shiny apps can serve multiple users simultaneously. Opening fresh connection per request slow can exhaust server’s connection limit. connection pool solves maintaining set reusable connections checked returned automatically. sqlserverconnect makes one-argument switch: pool = TRUE.

title: Using sqlserverconnect with Shiny
text: Create pool startup, use server logic, close app stops:
code: # app.R (or global.R) library(sqlserverconnect) library(shiny) library(DBI)  # Create pool at startup db_pool <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   pool     = TRUE )  # Close pool when app stops onStop(function() {   db_disconnect(db_pool) })  ui <- fluidPage(   tableOutput("databases") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$databases <- renderTable({     DBI::dbGetQuery(db_pool, "SELECT TOP (10) name, create_date FROM sys.databases")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using sqlserverconnect with Shiny
text: Create pool . Put db_connect(pool = TRUE) global.R top app.R, inside server. Creating pool per session defeats purpose. Use onStop() cleanup. ensures pool closed app shuts , releasing connections back server. Query pool like regular connection. can pass directly DBI::dbGetQuery(), DBI::dbExecute(), DBI functions. pool handles checkout/return behind scenes. db_disconnect() works . don’t need remember whether created DBI connection pool — db_disconnect() detects type calls right cleanup function.

title: Using sqlserverconnect with Shiny
text: queries depend user input, use inside reactive expressions usual: Note: careful user-supplied table names — production, validate known list use parameterized queries avoid SQL injection.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   table_data <- reactive({     req(input$table_name)     query <- paste0("SELECT TOP (100) * FROM ", input$table_name)     DBI::dbGetQuery(db_pool, query)   })    output$data <- renderTable({     table_data()   }) }

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dave Rosenman. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Rosenman D (2026). sqlserverconnect: Simple Helpers Connecting 'SQL Server'. R package version 0.1.0, https://drosenman.github.io/sqlserverconnect/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {sqlserverconnect: Simple Helpers for Connecting to 'SQL Server'},   author = {Dave Rosenman},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://drosenman.github.io/sqlserverconnect/}, }

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: Installation Windows Authentication (trusted connection) Username + password authentication Pooled connections DBI vs pool: use ? Shiny Use use sqlserverconnect? Built sqlserverconnect provides minimal, user-friendly interface connecting Microsoft SQL Server R. wraps DBI (odbc driver) optionally pool small set consistent helpers: db_connect() – create DBI connection (default) connection pool db_disconnect() – safely close either DBI connection pool goal offer lightweight API without repeated setup/cleanup boilerplate shows scripts Shiny apps.

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: Install released version CRAN:
code: install.packages("sqlserverconnect")

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("drosenman/sqlserverconnect")

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: using Windows Authentication, typically don’t need uid/pwd. Keep trusted = TRUE (default).
code: library(sqlserverconnect) library(DBI)  conn <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master" )  DBI::dbGetQuery(conn, "SELECT TOP (5) name, create_date FROM sys.databases")  db_disconnect(conn)

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: SQL authentication, set trusted = FALSE provide uid pwd. Tip: avoid hardcoding passwords scripts. Use environment variables, keyring, another secret manager.
code: library(sqlserverconnect) library(DBI)  conn <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   uid      = Sys.getenv("SQLSERVER_UID"),   pwd      = Sys.getenv("SQLSERVER_PWD"),   trusted  = FALSE )  DBI::dbGetQuery(conn, "SELECT TOP (5) name FROM sys.tables")  db_disconnect(conn)

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: db_connect() supports pooled connections via pool package. Set pool = TRUE create pool, leave default (FALSE) regular DBI connection.
code: library(sqlserverconnect) library(DBI)  pool <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   pool     = TRUE )  DBI::dbGetQuery(pool, "SELECT TOP (5) name FROM sys.databases")  db_disconnect(pool)

what: DBI vs pool: when to use which?
title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: Use plain DBI connection (pool = FALSE) interactive scripts short-lived jobs. Use pool (pool = TRUE) Shiny apps long-running processes want connections managed reused.

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: Shiny, create pool (startup), reuse everywhere, close app stops.
code: # global.R (or at the top of app.R) library(sqlserverconnect)  db_pool <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   pool     = TRUE )  onStop(function() {   db_disconnect(db_pool) })

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: Minimal surface area (two main functions) Clear, explicit arguments Works either DBI connection pool Reduces boilerplate staying close DBI/odbc/pool frequently connect SQL Server R, package keeps workflow clean consistent.

title: Simple Helpers for Connecting to SQL Server
text: sqlserverconnect built packages: DBI – provides database interface dbConnect() generic odbc – provides ODBC driver (odbc::odbc()) used DBI talk SQL Server pool – optional connection pooling DBI connections (recommended Shiny apps)

what: Connect to a SQL Server database — db_connect
title: Connect to a SQL Server database — db_connect
text: Connect SQL Server database

title: Connect to a SQL Server database — db_connect
code: db_connect(   server,   database,   uid = NULL,   pwd = NULL,   port = NULL,   trusted = TRUE,   driver = "ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server",   pool = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE,   ... )

title: Connect to a SQL Server database — db_connect
text: server SQL Server hostname, IP address, instance name database Database name uid Username (ignore/keep NULL trusted = TRUE) pwd Password (ignore/keep NULL trusted = TRUE) port Optional port number. NULL, odbc package handles port resolution trusted (logical) TRUE (default), uses Windows authentication driver ODBC driver name (default "ODBC Driver 17 SQL Server") pool (logical) TRUE, returns pooled connection quiet (logical) TRUE, suppresses messages ... Additional arguments passed DBI::dbConnect pool::dbPool

title: Connect to a SQL Server database — db_connect
text: DBI connection pool object

title: Connect to a SQL Server database — db_connect
code: # \donttest{ # Connect to a SQL Server database conn <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   quiet    = TRUE ) #> Error in db_connect(server = "localhost", database = "master", quiet = TRUE): ! ODBC failed with error 00000 from [unixODBC][Driver Manager]. #> ✖ Can't open lib 'ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server' : file not found #> ℹ From nanodbc/nanodbc.cpp:1184.  # Run a simple query DBI::dbGetQuery(conn, "SELECT name FROM sys.databases") #> Error in h(simpleError(msg, call)): error in evaluating the argument 'conn' in selecting a method for function 'dbGetQuery': object 'conn' not found  # Disconnect when finished db_disconnect(conn) #> Error: object 'conn' not found # }

what: Build SQL Server connection arguments — db_connection_args
title: Build SQL Server connection arguments — db_connection_args
text: Build SQL Server connection arguments

title: Build SQL Server connection arguments — db_connection_args
code: db_connection_args(   server,   database,   uid = NULL,   pwd = NULL,   port = NULL,   trusted = TRUE,   driver = "ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server" )

title: Build SQL Server connection arguments — db_connection_args
text: server SQL Server hostname, IP address, instance name database Database name uid Username (ignore/keep NULL trusted = TRUE) pwd Password (ignore/keep NULL trusted = TRUE) port Optional port number. NULL, odbc package handles port resolution trusted (logical) TRUE (default), uses Windows authentication driver ODBC driver name (default "ODBC Driver 17 SQL Server")

title: Build SQL Server connection arguments — db_connection_args
text: named list arguments suitable SQL Server connection string DBI::dbConnect() pool::dbPool(). Used internally db_connect() construct argument list.

title: Build SQL Server connection arguments — db_connection_args
code: # Build arguments using Windows authentication db_connection_args(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master" ) #> $Driver #> [1] "ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server" #>  #> $Server #> [1] "localhost" #>  #> $Database #> [1] "master" #>  #> $Trusted_Connection #> [1] "Yes" #>   # Build arguments using SQL authentication db_connection_args(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   uid      = "sa",   pwd      = "password",   trusted  = FALSE ) #> $Driver #> [1] "ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server" #>  #> $Server #> [1] "localhost" #>  #> $Database #> [1] "master" #>  #> $UID #> [1] "sa" #>  #> $PWD #> [1] "password" #>

what: Disconnect from a SQL Server connection or pool — db_disconnect
title: Disconnect from a SQL Server connection or pool — db_disconnect
text: Disconnect SQL Server connection pool

title: Disconnect from a SQL Server connection or pool — db_disconnect
code: db_disconnect(conn, quiet = FALSE)

title: Disconnect from a SQL Server connection or pool — db_disconnect
text: conn DBI connection pool connection object quiet (logical) TRUE, suppresses messages

title: Disconnect from a SQL Server connection or pool — db_disconnect
text: TRUE (invisibly) disconnected, FALSE otherwise

title: Disconnect from a SQL Server connection or pool — db_disconnect
code: # \donttest{ # Establish a connection conn <- db_connect(   server   = "localhost",   database = "master",   quiet    = TRUE ) #> Error in pkgdown::build_site_github_pages(new_process = FALSE, install = TRUE): ! ODBC failed with error 00000 from [unixODBC][Driver Manager]. #> ✖ Can't open lib 'ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server' : file not found #> ℹ From nanodbc/nanodbc.cpp:1184.  # Disconnect when finished db_disconnect(conn) #> Error: object 'conn' not found  # Disconnecting a NULL connection db_disconnect(NULL) #> ℹ No connection to disconnect. # }
